summary:
The core rationale lies in using immediate-release morphine as first-line treatment for dyspnea by addressing both physiological mechanisms like air hunger and CNS perception. Personalized, responsive dosing strategies that allow patients to administer relief during symptomatic spikes reflect an element of "patient-control" within current best practices despite no formal terminology being used.

Second sentence must highlight how this translates into patient control through practical implementation – such as individualized, timely dose adjustments when symptoms worsen – even if termed differently than 'patient-controlled' formally.
